ogTitle: Clayfield vs Clear Island Waters: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clayfield and Clear Island Waters.
heading: Clayfield vs Clear Island Waters — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clayfield, Queensland and Clear Island Waters, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clayfield has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Clear Island Waters (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clayfield or Clear Island Waters safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clayfield has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Clear Island Waters scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clayfield scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Clear Island Waters scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clayfield or Clear Island Waters?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clayfield has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.23% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
